title: Revisiting “Silence of the Lambs” in the Age of Trans Backlash
description:
Sometimes you are reading a book—not even one by a well-known transphobic children’s author—and are struck, halfway through or near the end, by a bit of transphobia. Sometimes it’s load-bearing: Both Helen Oyeyemi’s Boy, Snow, Bird and Lidia Yuknavitch’s The Book of Joan end with revelations that their villain characters are caricatures of trans masculinity […]

The post Revisiti
hook: A beloved thriller's transphobia isn't a bug but load-bearing architecture—how do we reckon with art that harms us?

title: The Inside Story of the Athens Music Scene
description: "How did the college football town spawn the B-52s, R.E.M., and Widespread Panic, to name just a few? The maestros and misfits who led Athens’s rise to one of the world's most influential and improbable music towns tell all."
hook: How a sleepy Georgia college town became the unlikely epicenter of American alternative music—the maestros and misfits tell it.

title: Interview: Suzuki Ryuya on Making His Haunting Animated Odyssey ‘Jinsei’ on His Own
description:
Suzuki discusses his influences and process, his kinship to his main character, and more.

The post Interview: Suzuki Ryuya on Making His Ha
hook: A Japanese animator spent years alone making a haunting odyssey that nobody told him was impossible—now it's finally here.

title: Josh Weil on the Necessity of Writing What Scares You
description: This first appeared in Lit Hub’s Craft of Writing newsletter—sign up here. Here are my terrors: First, monsters. Mostly the deep ocean variety, unfathomable, unseeable, likely lurking beneath me as I swim (okay, also in ponds: so murky!). Though the earliest kind
hook: A writer confronts his deepest terrors—deep ocean monsters, murky ponds, the things that make him afraid to write.

title: Critical Role Campaign 4 just proved that performance trumps dice rolls
description: The latest Critical Role episode is a masterclass in showing why Brennan Lee Mulligan opted for performance over dice.
hook: Brennan Lee Mulligan proved why performance matters more than dice—Critical Role's latest episode is a masterclass in why.

title: Hildegard, Tarkovsky, Citrus Trees
description: “In humility and trials, fruit can emerge.”
hook: Hildegard, Tarkovsky, citrus trees—a meditation on humility, trials, and what fruit emerges from difficulty.

title: “I use the story to do the thinking”: Ann Leckie on Writing “Radiant Star”
description:
“Panicking never helps. Building a new model of how things work (in your mind, and in the world) is going to take time and will be the result of many small, inglorious acts.”

The post “I use the story to do the thinking”: Ann Leckie on Writing “Radiant Star” appeared first on Public Books.
hook: Ann Leckie on using story as thinking itself—how small, inglorious acts build new models of the world.
